heading: Amounts reserved; allotments
label: 9859c Amounts reserved; allotments

Russell National School Lunch Act, , , which is classified generally to chapter 13 (§ 1751 et seq.) of this title. children.heading: Amounts reserved
children.label: a Amounts reserved
children.text: The Secretary shall reserve not more than one-half of 1 percent of the amount appropriated under for each fiscal year to make allotments to Guam, American Samoa, the United States Virgin Islands, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands to be allotted in accordance with their respective needs.section 9859a of this title

children.label: A
children.text: an amount that bears the same ratio to 50 percent of such remainder as the product of the young child factor of the State and the allotment percentage of the State bears to the sum of the corresponding products for all States; and

children.label: B
children.text: an amount that bears the same ratio to 50 percent of such remainder as the product of the school lunch factor of the State and the allotment percentage of the State bears to the sum of the corresponding products for all States.

children.heading: Young child factor
children.label: 2 Young child factor
children.text: In this subsection, the term “young child factor” means the ratio of the number of children under 5 years of age in a State to the number of such children in all States, as provided by the most recent annual estimates of population in the States by the Census Bureau of the Department of Commerce.

children.heading: School lunch factor
children.label: 3 School lunch factor
children.text: In this subsection, the term “school lunch factor” means the ratio of the number of children who are receiving free or reduced price lunches under the school lunch program established under the National School Lunch Act ( et seq.) in the State to the number of such children in all States, as determined annually by the Department of Agriculture.42 U.S.C. 1751

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the allotment percentage for a State shall be determined by dividing the per capita income of all individuals in the United States, by the per capita income of all individuals in the State.

children.label: i
children.text: is more than 1.2 percent, the allotment percentage of the State shall be considered to be 1.2 percent; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is less than 0.8 percent, the allotment percentage of the State shall be considered to be 0.8 percent.

children.label: ii
children.text: applied for the 2-year period beginning on October 1 of the first fiscal year beginning after the date such determination is made; and

children.label: iii
children.text: equal to the average of the annual per capita incomes for the most recent period of 3 consecutive years for which satisfactory data are available from the Department of Commerce on the date such determination is made.

children.heading: Data and information
children.label: c Data and information
children.text: The Secretary shall obtain from each appropriate Federal agency, the most recent data and information necessary to determine the allotments provided for in subsection (b).

children.heading: Definition
children.label: d Definition
children.text: In this section, the term “State” includes only the several States of the United States, the District of Columbia, and the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico.
